title: Dremio
description: Open lakehouse platform with a SQL query engine and self-service semantic layer over data lakes, databases, and object storage.
installation: This template deploys a SINGLE-NODE Dremio Community Edition (dremio-oss) running coordinator + executor in one pod, which is the supported way to self-host/evaluate the OSS engine. Dremio OSS is self-contained: it stores its metadata KV store (RocksDB), uploads, and accelerator/cache files on LOCAL DISK under /opt/dremio/data, so NO database add-on is provisioned -- a persistent volume is mounted there instead and MUST remain persistent or you lose all sources, spaces, and reflections. FIRST RUN: open the web UI on port 9047, the very first form creates the admin account and password -- set a strong password there (nothing to rotate in this template). PORTS: 9047 = HTTP web UI / REST API (the containerPort published here); the engine also listens on 31010 (Dremio ODBC/native client + coordinator-executor RPC), 32010 (Apache Arrow Flight, used by Python/JDBC Arrow clients), and 45678 (inter-node coordination) -- expose those via additional Services if external clients/BI tools need them. SCALING: to run a real distributed cluster you deploy SEPARATE COMPANION apps from the SAME image `dremio/dremio-oss` configured as dedicated roles -- (1) a coordinator-only node (DREMIO_JAVA_SERVER_EXTRA_OPTS with services.coordinator.enabled=true, services.executor.enabled=false), (2) one or more executor-only nodes (services.coordinator.enabled=false, services.executor.enabled=true) that register back to the coordinator over 31010/45678, and (3) an external distributed store (S3/HDFS) for the dist path instead of the local PVC; these companions are NOT created here. NOTE: Dremio OSS uses external object stores (S3/ADLS/GCS/HDFS) and JDBC sources for actual data -- those are configured at runtime in the UI, not as add-ons. Bump the app pod size (Dremio is a JVM and needs several GiB of heap) before loading real workloads.
